title: Harden crossbridge-server: close inherited file descriptors at startup
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

## Context

The direnv helper (`nix/direnvrc.sh`) launches `crossbridge-server` detached. A
bug there leaked direnv's `.envrc` evaluation pipe (fd 3) into the long-lived
server: direnv never saw EOF on that pipe, so the shell that triggered `.envrc`
hung until the server exited. Fixed launcher-side by adding `3>&-` to the
detached launch.

That fix is launcher-specific. This issue is the defense-in-depth complement:
make `crossbridge-server` itself drop any inherited file descriptors at startup,
so it is robust against *any* launcher (Makefile, CI wrapper, shell script, ...)
that leaks an fd.

## Why this is safe for this server

`crossbridge-server` never legitimately inherits a file descriptor:

- It **connects out** to the supervisor (`UnixStream::connect`, `supervisor.rs`).
- It **creates** its own per-peer listening sockets (`listeners.rs`).
- It opens its own crosslink DB, tokio epoll fd, and log sink.
- `.design/overview.md`: the server is "started manually in tmux (not systemd)"
  — no socket activation, no fd-passing anywhere in the design.

Every fd >= 3 the process starts with is therefore a launcher leak.

## What to implement

In `crossbridge-server/src/main.rs`, as the **first statement of `main()`** —
before `tracing_subscriber`, before `Cli::parse()`, before the tokio runtime
builds its epoll fd — close every inherited fd >= 3:

```rust
// Drop every inherited fd >= 3 before we open any of our own. A launcher
// (e.g. a direnv .envrc) may leak its evaluation pipe to us; holding it
// open hangs the launcher until this long-lived process exits.
```

- Add `libc` as a direct dependency of `crossbridge-server` (already transitive
  via tokio; one Cargo.toml line, no compile cost).
- Use `close_range(2)` — a single atomic syscall, no iteration races. It
  requires Linux 5.9+. If `close_range` returns `ENOSYS`, fall back to
  iterating `/proc/self/fd` and closing each entry >= 3.
- Leave fds 0/1/2 untouched — fd 1/2 carry `tracing` output; fd 0 is always
  valid as launched.

## Caveat to document in the code comment

Closing inherited fds would break systemd socket activation (which passes
sockets starting at fd 3 with `$LISTEN_FDS` set). The server has no
socket-activation path today, but the comment must call this out so a future
change is aware. Optional: skip the close when `$LISTEN_FDS` is set.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `crossbridge-server` closes all inherited fds >= 3 as the first action in `main()`.
- [ ] Implemented with `close_range`, with a `/proc/self/fd` fallback on `ENOSYS`.
- [ ] fds 0/1/2 are left intact.
- [ ] A test verifies an fd opened before `exec` of the server is not held open
      by the running server (e.g. spawn the server with an extra inherited pipe
      fd, assert the write end reaches EOF / the fd is absent from
      `/proc/<pid>/fd`).
- [ ] `cargo test`, `cargo clippy`, `cargo fmt` all clean.
- [ ] The `3>&-` fix in `nix/direnvrc.sh` stays — this is complementary, not a replacement.
